title: Align .rules and zed-agentops-init.sh with startup contract
summary: Update the rule template and initialization script so the generated startup guidance and initialized artifacts match the zero-event baseline contract used by runtime code.
outputs: Consistent initialization behavior for zero-event baseline startup
acceptance_criteria: The generated .rules content clearly documents that an empty canonical event log is a valid initialized baseline.
The generated .rules content remains consistent with strict transaction ordering while describing how initial planning work may bootstrap correctly.
zed-agentops-init.sh creates or preserves initialization artifacts in a way that matches the documented startup contract.
The initialization script and the checked-in .rules file do not contradict Python runtime assumptions about startup, restore order, or derived artifacts.
Changes are covered by regression tests that exercise script-content expectations and startup contract alignment.
tasks: Review the checked-in .rules file and the rule template embedded in zed-agentops-init.sh for startup-contract drift.
Update startup, restore-order, and task-lifecycle guidance so the initial zero-event baseline is described precisely.
Ensure the script-generated rules text matches the canonical repository .rules content for the relevant startup behavior.
Verify that initialization behavior for canonical and derived .agent artifacts matches runtime expectations.
Add or update tests that lock the alignment between script content, rule content, and startup contract language.
notes: Do not weaken strict transaction ordering globally; only clarify and align bootstrap behavior for the initial baseline.
Treat .agent/handoff.json as derived-only and keep canonical transaction files as the source of truth.
Keep the script and rule text concise, explicit, and stable enough for automated planning and resume flows.
